item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent series of edits was reverted, as they were more stylistic changes than corrections and thus need permission from the original author.

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I've gone ahead and restored it.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
From Zmax's Author Page:

This was pretty quick to write, with most of the edits coming in about the ending. The tree in the photo is visible from my window, and that’s about it.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: There is no indication that the image was ever released under CC 3.0 and is assumed non-compliant. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I'd thought of that.

EDIT: Until today I didn't realize this skip had been featured. All of my thanks to the SCP admin team, who've been doing a pretty solid job for many years!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it overall, but the end confused me. 13 Meters doesn't sound like much, so disappearing out of view by simply climbing doesn't make sense to me.

Also missed some kind of test via crane to get to the top, seemed like an obvious test to make, but i maybe just misunderstood some part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it a lot, the idea was very creative, and I have to admit, I would not have thought of making an SCP like this one. Quite unique and good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Yeah that uh. That would’ve been really actually quite good. Radiation poisoning, and signs of trouble breathing due to altitude, would have felt much better than “killed”. And then instead of “at speed exceeding terminal velocity”, it could’ve said just “at terminal velocity”, suggesting they fell from a very great height. I think that would’ve been better than the current ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the description was setup for something really cool in the addendum. I especially loved the “feeling strangely heavy”. But the addendum felt like nothing, which means your build went nowhere. The “and it killed a guy” felt shoe-horned. Don’t know what I was hoping for, but it wasn’t that. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is really well put together!
A very interesting scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting idea! Good job!

item.title: puns
item.content:encoded:
wow.
just

wow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"hey guess what tree, i just used a pulley to climb you! SUCK I-."

*tree snaps neck*

No uncollapsed Zalgo text on the forums please
hide

.
.
A̝̻͕̰̤͔͕̎̏ͮ̍ͨͣ̒ͯ͡ͅR̳͍̔̀ͤͣE̓̓̈́͋͛̏̔̚̚͏̳̲̭̯̤ ̢̫̪̻͚͚͓̩̎̓͋̑̾̔̔ͅY̼̬̖̗̍ͫ͛ͦǑ̷̩̜͔͍̩͎͓̐͆̏͒ͩ̑ͧ̉͜͝Ŭ̵̥͚̒̄ͥ͂ͯ͜ ̡̛̖̱̝̤͑̑ͤS̸͔̗̺͈̙͈̭ͩͣ̄͒͂͐̔ͦ͝U͙̻̹͙̐̿̓͒̒ͤ̑̄ͨ̕͜R͔̩͖̬̱͛̂̑̊̾̃E͍̰̘̝̙͎̻̳ͨͫ͗͂̉̀̚ͅ ͚͙̦͈̫͛̌ͯ͊ͦ̍̂͝Ả̛͖̙͙̗̙͈̝͇̓͡B̢͔̘̖͎̝̮̎ͭͨ͒ͥͪ̎ͮ͜͜ͅỎ̼̤̳̜͕͉͖̬̮́̊ͪ̔̈͢͝͡Ủ̪̫͑̾̄͛̕͜Tͫ́̆ͤ̕͏̤̖ ̖̰̠̝̺͔̹̗̅͗̇̽T̲͔͔̱ͬ͗̽̀̑͊ͬ̕͡ͅH̡̻̞͓̥̳͚̥͚ͦͦ͢͜Ã̠͓̺̘͙̪̊ͣT̑̌ͪ͆͑̾̂̀͏̛̹̣̙͎̲̣̠

what am i doing with myself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think i’d have swapped the hanging with obvious starvation and oxygen deprivation, suggesting he kept climbing until he couldn’t. Hell, add radiation exposure to really boost the altitude…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somewhere, a GOC spy is crying because "Y'all just *had* to try to outsmart the fucking unclimbable tree."

That said, if it only stops humans, strapping a camera to a squirrel would probably either be very interesting or very boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Mr. Wrong. It makes me want to add a machine exploration log to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A simple but fun scp that goes from interesting to kinda creepy. Overall, a nice read! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late suggestion: It currently says "No changes in weight or gravity have ever been observed". It would be more precise to say "No changes in mass or gravity have ever been observed". Because w = mg.

Otherwise, good stuff. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A nice short and simple one. I am curious what would happen though if someone tried to land on the top of the tree via a rope from a helicopter. Would they simply vanish like what happened in the incident log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a good thing there's only one of these. After all, tree's a crowd.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet. Upvoted.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
It's got a good, creepy vibe. Bravo!

https://youtu.be/4tr236LNFSA

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha! I love how little we (i.e., the Foundation) can determine from observation. I'm certain the author knew exactly what happened to the tree/happens to climbers, but there's really no way for us to know for sure (I'm certain video recordings would be damaged/destroyed in a fall like that). I love it.

I loved it. It's a simple yet well executed idea that leaves your mind racing as to its origin. The incident report leaves you puzzled, in a good way, about why it, or maybe something in it (possibly who/whatever put the ladder rungs on the tree) is killing people who make it up. And even though it isn't really an accomplishment in any sense of the word, I feel oddly proud to be the one who's vote brought it to 100.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like how this is an "early in-universe" snapshot of a document. Later versions would have conducted testing using remote controlled/surveillance equipment. Tests were done in 89, don't blame them

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roughly two hours later

had died several days prior

Shouldn't there be a temporal tag on this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the description of the object to be a bit bland. It seemed odd that nobody could climb the tree but it wasn't something that seemed interesting beyond that, until the incident log at the bottom though. That's what made this SCP interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My pleasure!

I'm here to defend trees, you see. If at all possible, mature trees should NOT be moved. They don't like that!

Signed: Tree-Hugger ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This similar in concept to SCP-1932 but I think it is different enough and interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one could use a little more to it. As it stands now, it's a straight up tree that you can't climb. I might have been okay with neutral-voting it if it were just that, but there's also this element of menace introduced with "something really doesn't want people to climb it and will kill you if you do". You're introducing an actor of some sort with absolutely nothing to it except that it will kill people that climb the tree. So now it feels like a weird phenomenon with no real deeper interest, except also with unnecessary elements added in. The thing that kills people feels like a half-measure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno, maybe. Come to think of it, it seems somewhat extraneous. I'll probably remove it. Thanks for the comment, and the the vote too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wasn't sure at first, but the more I read and think about it, the better I like it.

I wonder why you wrote this:

Originally located in █████ █████, ██████████

Does it change anything to the skip? Just askin'!

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just an incredibly fun read! It's simple, but that's good. That's not, of course, an insult. The piece really does have a Series I feel without a lot of what makes Series I scips bad in hindsight (poor writing). +1, obviously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds a lot of the endless rope to nowhere back in Series I, but it's got a real Series I feel to it, and it's just tantalizing as heck. That test really sells it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is the reason why our parents told us not to climb trees when they were younger… Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good progression from strange, to weird, to creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure whether to leave it at neutral or to upvote. It's honestly a bit non-sequitur.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:

  • To be honest, the first paragraph led me on and serves as a good hook to it.
  • The anomaly is simple yet interesting.

Cons:

  • If anything, the last part feels like a cliffhanger and I feel like there's more to it that meets the eye.

Verdict: Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple and mysterious. I like it a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool and totally unpredictable. Nice job my friend!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the draft, still love it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple anomaly leads into creepy+unexplained territory. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome.
